turns.content: okay I need your help with something that is not architecture for once. Diego turns 50 next month and I have absolutely no idea what to give him. twenty-two years of marriage and the man still surprises me with his ability to want nothing
Ha, the classic hard-to-buy-for husband. When you say he wants nothing, is he genuinely minimalist, or does he just not ask for things? What does he actually spend his free time doing?

he is genuinely like this. the man owns three pairs of shoes and thinks that is excessive. his free time — he reads history and engineering journals, he plays guitar very badly but with enormous joy, and he likes wine in a quiet unpretentious way. he is not a man of objects. he is a man of routines and small pleasures

That gives me something to work with. Since he's not materialistic, a few directions:

- **Experience over object** — a weekend trip somewhere, a private guitar lesson with a real musician, a special dinner.
- **A handmade guitar** — if he plays with joy even badly, a beautiful Chilean luthier-made guitar could be deeply meaningful.
- **A rare book** — given the history and engineering interests, a first edition or special printing of something significant.

Which direction feels most like him?

the guitar idea — you know that is actually brilliant. he has this old guitar he bought at a feria when we were dating, it is held together by stubbornness and love at this point. a handmade one would make him cry. Diego does not cry often but when he does it is like watching a cathedral leak

"Like watching a cathedral leak" — that's a beautiful image. A handmade classical guitar sounds like the right call then. Do you know what kind he plays — nylon string classical or steel string? And do you have a budget in mind? Chilean luthiers range from about 200,000 to well over a million pesos.

classical, nylon strings, always. budget — look, the man has endured two decades of me talking about cornices and mortar ratios. he deserves whatever it costs. maybe up to 800,000 pesos. I want it to be special but I am not losing my mind entirely

800,000 is a great budget for a handmade classical guitar. Look into Alfredo Lagos in Santiago — he makes concert-quality instruments with Chilean tonewoods. Ask him if he has any completed guitars in stock given your timeline. Cedar tops tend to be warmer and more forgiving, which is perfect for someone who plays for joy. And ask about a hardshell case — important for protecting a good instrument.

cedar top, warm and forgiving — that sounds like Diego himself honestly. I will call this week. yalla, if this guitar makes him cry I will send you a recording of the cathedral leaking. thank you habibi
I would love to hear that cathedral leak. Good luck with the luthier search — I think this is going to be a really special gift. Let me know how it goes.
